verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (TGN46/TGN51) is a type I integral membrane protein that resides constitutively in the trans-Golgi network and cycles between early endosomes and the TGN. Its lumenal/ectodomain faces the Golgi lumen, not the extracellular space. There is no documented stable plasma membrane pool, no shed ectodomain with therapeutic relevance, no GPI anchor, no CD designation, and no HGNC surface-protein family membership. It is an endomembrane-resident TM protein with no credible outer-leaflet surface story.
